item.title: Because it is what badasses do! :)
item.dc:creator: Luvlyquants

item.title: About to get fired...
item.description:
Context: I work 12 hour shifts as a security guard at a hospital. I really like a lot of my coworkers. And they tolerate my bad dad jokes. My relief didn't show up. Apparently, my coworker gave a 7 hour advanced notice the night before. I stayed an extra 2 hours with still no one showing to relieve me. This means the company had roughly 9 hours to find a replacement for me. I began falling asleep at the security desk. I relieved myself from work because I felt unfit for duty. Now my boss is saying I abandoned my post and took me off the schedule. He told me to bring in my uniforms at the main office too so we could "talk." This was the 3rd time my relief hadn't shown. The last 2 times it happened, the other officers also called out in advanced like this time. The job is very easy work, but the pay is horrible. I live in a state in the USA where it's at-will employment, and we're not a part of a union. *sigh* I won't miss my boss, but I will miss all my coworkers. I'm just screaming into the void.
Wild bunny tax.
item.dc:creator: AeonexAshaxei

item.title: What happened to science guy?
item.description:
Just came back after a long break and wanted to check this week in science update
Last post about 1 year ago :(
Hope he is doing allright, if anyone of you guys know why he stopped posting feel free to tell me :c #sad #science #midly_interesting

item.description: Or gouging them out. Jesus was rather clear about that, I think.
item.dc:creator: Morganelefay

item.title: It's a very small win but still technically a win
item.description: Just a short phone interview for a couple hours of teaching per week at almost half my normal rate. But I'll take it if they offer. FP edit: Thanks for the (up)votes of confidence! I hope it's not bad form to add that if anyone is interested in brushing up on their conversational or business French skills, my rates are reasonable and I will still have availability over the summer, even if I get this job. My background is mostly working with kids K-12 in French Immersion and Core French but I've been teaching adults as well for the last few years and I really enjoy it.
item.dc:creator: ImmortalChild

item.title: Such a cruel world.
item.description:
He lays on me when he's either very content, or wants something. I think he's just happy because I gave him a little extra breakfast. Wish I could just lay here, but their kibble isn't gonna pay for itself.
He's actually lost some weight, been on a diet since January, but it's been hell on us all lol
item.dc:creator: nomorehate

item.title: One of us shared his personal experience, and asks that we hold our loved ones close.
item.description: @JKBenton you are one of my favorite imgur artists, and loved by many. I'm so glad your son is recovering. I hope by sharing your story people will drive more carefully, and keep an eye out for pedestrian and bike riders; especially at crosswalks! I don't go on insta often, but came across this today.

item.dc:creator: DerpMeister

item.title: Signal boost and help catch a murderer
item.description: Dear Community Member On Wednesday night, June 5th at 10:00 pm my only brother Luis Manuel Arguello-Inglis was murdered sitting on a bench in Dolores Park on 20th st between Church and Dolores He is my entire world, my best friend, my big baby brother, Luis had just finished his EMT training and his first year at San Jose State; he was on his path to be a firefighter so he could help people. I am reaching out to see if you saw or heard anything that might help our family find peace. If you have any security cameras on your home, please help us find justice for my sweet Luis by checking any video records from the evening of June 5th. Please, please share any information that you have with me and the investigators. ANYTHING is helpful My email: justiceforluisarguello@gmail.com SFPD Investigators email: brent.sullivan@sfgov.org SFPD Tip Line call 1-415-575-4444 or Text a Tip to TIP411 and begin the text message with SFPD.
item.dc:creator: ReadyPlayerTwoxx

item.title: Fresh(ish) Memes #145
item.dc:creator: ShaggyRoots

item.title: Chicken Parm Meatballs
item.description:
“I want meatballs!” “I want chicken Parm!” Everyone loves a compromise
Cross section EDIT: Because y’all keep asking 3 ounces panko bread crumbs 4 ounces Parmesan cheese, grated 1.5lb ground chicken 1 egg, lightly beaten 2 tablespoons olive oil 1 tablespoon dried basil 2 teaspoons garlic powder 2 teaspoons dried parsley 1 teaspoon dried oregano 1 teaspoon kosher salt Whatever marinara sauce your heart desires 4 ounces mozzarella cheese, grated 1. Heat the oven to 400°F. 2. Mix together the panko and the Parmesan cheese 3. Combine the chicken, egg, 1 tablespoon of the oil, ~3 ounces panko/Parmesan, the basil, garlic powder, parsley, oregano and salt in a large bowl. Using your hands, gently combine until just incorporated, trying not to overwork the meat. 4. Divide the meatball mixture into meatballs ~1oz each and roll them in the panko mixture. 5. Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon oil over medium heat in an oven-safe skillet. Brown the meatballs in the oil. Remove the browned meatballs from the pan and add the sauce to the pan. 6. Add the meatballs back to the pan, cover with the grated mozzarella and bake until the meatballs are cooked through, about 10-15 minutes, and the cheese is melted. Serve over cooked pasta or on top of crispy bread.
item.dc:creator: Gorglax9

item.dc:creator: V75TheVoid

item.title: 7 weeks of no drinking.
item.description: And right now I'm kind of going through a difficult time. If nothing serious. But still. In the past I would have immediately started drinking. And I just keep telling myself that doing so will just make it worse so why do it? In the meantime I've gotten into some different things that I previously did not care about at all. Like I've taken up gardening. Even though it's very difficult where I live because it's mostly sand and rocks and cactuses but whatever. It keeps me busy. Just another week and it'll be 2 months which I feel is a semi milestone so yay me.
item.dc:creator: oldradios19772000

item.title: The goodest gorl.
item.description: So, I'll preface this with, I'm allergic to dogs. I did not want a dog. So now I have a dog. Her name's Sitka. My life fell apart for the billionth time a few days before Christmas, after which I drove across a few states to hole up in an RV in the mountains, because I was broke. Ended up taking care of a dog who's person (my step mom - we had a really strained relationship) passed in June. Then taking on full responsibility so her next person (my dad) didn't have to give her up, given he flies and couldn't give her the care she needed. So now I have a dog. And holy shit I would not trade her for all the Claritin in the world. I love her to bits. EDIT** AAAAAAAAAAAH. OH JEEZ IT GOT POPULAR. I'm on fexofenadine and famotidine as a secondary antihistamine! And yes!! Exposure really has helped! First month was a month of not breathing very well at all. Now I can let her hide in my bed during thunder storms! HEPA air purifier helps too!!! Lol
item.dc:creator: milostop
